NKJV.1: “Let  not your heart be troubled; you believe in God, believe also in Me.
NKJV.2: In My Father’s house are many mansions; if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
NKJV.3: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and receive you to Myself; that where I am, there you may be also.
NKJV.4: And where I go you know, and the way you know.”
NKJV.5: Thomas said to Him, “Lord, we do not know where You are going, and how can we know the way?”
NKJV.6: Jesus said to him, “I am  the way,  the truth, and  the life.  No one comes to the Father  except through Me.
NKJV.7: “If you had known Me, you would have known My Father also; and from now on you know Him and have seen Him.”
NKJV.8: Philip said to Him, “Lord, show us the Father, and it is sufficient for us.”
NKJV.9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you so long, and yet you have not known Me, Philip?  He who has seen Me has seen the Father; so how can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
NKJV.10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father, and the Father in Me? The words that I speak to you I do not speak on My own authority; but the Father who dwells in Me does the works.
NKJV.11: Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father in Me, or else believe Me for the sake of the works themselves.
NKJV.12: “Most assuredly, I say to you, he who believes in Me, the works that I do he will do also; and greater works than these he will do, because I go to My Father.
NKJV.13: And whatever you ask in My name, that I will do, that the Father may be  glorified in the Son.
NKJV.14: If you ask anything in My name, I will do it.
NKJV.15: “If you love Me, keep My commandments.
NKJV.16: And I will pray the Father, and  He will give you another Helper, that He may abide with you forever—
NKJV.17: the Spirit of truth,  whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees Him nor knows Him; but you know Him, for He dwells with you  and will be in you.
NKJV.18: I will not leave you orphans;  I will come to you.
NKJV.19: “A little while longer and the world will see Me no more, but  you will see Me.  Because I live, you will live also.
NKJV.20: At that day you will know that I am in My Father, and you in Me, and I in you.
NKJV.21: He who has My commandments and keeps them, it is he who loves Me. And he who loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I will love him and manifest Myself to him.”
NKJV.22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to Him, “Lord, how is it that You will manifest Yourself to us, and not to the world?”
NKJV.23: Jesus answered and said to him, “If anyone loves Me, he will keep My word; and My Father will love him,  and We will come to him and make Our home with him.
NKJV.24: He who does not love Me does not keep My words; and  the word which you hear is not Mine but the Father’s who sent Me.
NKJV.25: “These things I have spoken to you while being present with you.
NKJV.26: But  the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will  send in My name,  He will teach you all things, and bring to your  remembrance all things that I said to you.
NKJV.27: Peace I leave with you, My peace I give to you; not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
NKJV.28: You have heard Me say to you, ‘I am going away and coming back to you.’ If you loved Me, you would rejoice because I said, ‘I am going to the Father,’ for My Father is greater than I.
NKJV.29: “And  now I have told you before it comes, that when it does come to pass, you may believe.
NKJV.30: I will no longer talk much with you,  for the ruler of this world is coming, and he has  nothing in Me.
NKJV.31: But that the world may know that I love the Father, and  as the Father gave Me commandment, so I do. Arise, let us go from here.

ESV.1: “Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God;  believe also in me.
ESV.2: In my Father's house are many rooms. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?
ESV.3: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself, that where I am you may be also.
ESV.4: And you know the way to where I am going.”
ESV.5: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we do not know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
ESV.6: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
ESV.7: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also.  From now on you do know him and have seen him.”
ESV.8: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and it is enough for us.”
ESV.9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you so long, and you still do not know me, Philip? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
ESV.10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on my own authority, but the Father who dwells in me does his works.
ESV.11: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me, or else believe on account of the works themselves.
ESV.12: “Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes in me will also do the works that I do; and greater works than these will he do, because I am going to the Father.
ESV.13: Whatever you ask in my name, this I will do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
ESV.14: If you ask me  anything in my name, I will do it.
ESV.15: “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
ESV.16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Helper,  to be with you forever,
ESV.17: even the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him. You know him, for he dwells with you and will be  in you.
ESV.18: “I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
ESV.19: Yet a little while and the world will see me no more, but you will see me. Because I live, you also will live.
ESV.20: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
ESV.21: Whoever has my commandments and keeps them, he it is who loves me. And he who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and manifest myself to him.”
ESV.22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, how is it that you will manifest yourself to us, and not to the world?”
ESV.23: Jesus answered him,  “If anyone loves me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him.
ESV.24: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words. And the word that you hear is not mine but the Father's who sent me.
ESV.25: “These things I have spoken to you while I am still with you.
ESV.26: But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things and bring to your remembrance all that I have said to you.
ESV.27: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.
ESV.28: You heard me say to you, ‘I am going away, and I will come to you.’ If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I am going to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
ESV.29: And now I have told you before it takes place, so that when it does take place you may believe.
ESV.30: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no claim on me,
ESV.31: but I do as the Father has commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Rise, let us go from here.

NASB.1: 'Do not let your heart be troubled; believe in God, believe also in Me.
NASB.2: 'In My Father's house are many dwelling places; if it were not so, I would have told you; for I go to prepare a place for you.
NASB.3: 'If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and receive you to Myself, that where I am, [there] you may be also.
NASB.4: 'And you know the way where I am going.'
NASB.5: Thomas said to Him, 'Lord, we do not know where You are going, how do we know the way?'
NASB.6: Jesus said to him, 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life; no one comes to the Father but through Me.
NASB.7: 'If you had known Me, you would have known My Father also; from now on you know Him, and have seen Him.'
NASB.8: Philip said to Him, 'Lord, show us the Father, and it is enough for us.'
NASB.9: Jesus said to him, 'Have I been so long with you, and [yet] you have not come to know Me, Philip? He who has seen Me has seen the Father; how [can] you say, 'Show us the Father '?
NASB.10: 'Do you not believe that I am in the Father, and the Father is in Me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on My own initiative, but the Father abiding in Me does His works.
NASB.11: 'Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me; otherwise believe because of the works themselves.
NASB.12: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, he who believes in Me, the works that I do, he will do also; and greater [works] than these he will do; because I go to the Father.
NASB.13: 'Whatever you ask in My name, that will I do, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
NASB.14: 'If you ask Me anything in My name, I will do [it].
NASB.15: 'If you love Me, you will keep My commandments.
NASB.16: 'I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Helper, that He may be with you forever;
NASB.17: [that is] the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it does not see Him or know Him, [but] you know Him because He abides with you and will be in you.
NASB.18: 'I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
NASB.19: 'After a little while the world will no longer see Me, but you [will] see Me; because I live, you will live also.
NASB.20: 'In that day you will know that I am in My Father, and you in Me, and I in you.
NASB.21: 'He who has My commandments and keeps them is the one who loves Me; and he who loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I will love him and will disclose Myself to him.'
NASB.22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to Him, 'Lord, what then has happened that You are going to disclose Yourself to us and not to the world?'
NASB.23: Jesus answered and said to him, 'If anyone loves Me, he will keep My word; and My Father will love him, and We will come to him and make Our abode with him.
NASB.24: 'He who does not love Me does not keep My words; and the word which you hear is not Mine, but the Father's who sent Me.
NASB.25: 'These things I have spoken to you while abiding with you.
NASB.26: 'But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name, He will teach you all things, and bring to your remembrance all that I said to you.
NASB.27: 'Peace I leave with you; My peace I give to you; not as the world gives do I give to you. Do not let your heart be troubled, nor let it be fearful.
NASB.28: 'You heard that I said to you, 'I go away, and I will come to you.' If you loved Me, you would have rejoiced because I go to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
NASB.29: 'Now I have told you before it happens, so that when it happens, you may believe.
NASB.30: 'I will not speak much more with you, for the ruler of the world is coming, and he has nothing in Me;
NASB.31: but so that the world may know that I love the Father, I do exactly as the Father commanded Me. Get up, let us go from here.

NLT.1: “Don’t let your hearts be troubled. Trust in God, and trust also in me.
NLT.2: There is more than enough room in my Father’s home. If this were not so, would I have told you that I am going to prepare a place for you?
NLT.3: When everything is ready, I will come and get you, so that you will always be with me where I am.
NLT.4: And you know the way to where I am going.”
NLT.5: “No, we don’t know, Lord,” Thomas said. “We have no idea where you are going, so how can we know the way?”
NLT.6: Jesus told him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one can come to the Father except through me.
NLT.7: If you had really known me, you would know who my Father is. From now on, you do know him and have seen him!”
NLT.8: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father, and we will be satisfied.”
NLT.9: Jesus replied, “Have I been with you all this time, Philip, and yet you still don’t know who I am? Anyone who has seen me has seen the Father! So why are you asking me to show him to you?
NLT.10: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words I speak are not my own, but my Father who lives in me does his work through me.
NLT.11: Just believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me. Or at least believe because of the work you have seen me do.
NLT.12: “I tell you the truth, anyone who believes in me will do the same works I have done, and even greater works, because I am going to be with the Father.
NLT.13: You can ask for anything in my name, and I will do it, so that the Son can bring glory to the Father.
NLT.14: Yes, ask me for anything in my name, and I will do it!
NLT.15: “If you love me, obey my commandments.
NLT.16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate, who will never leave you.
NLT.17: He is the Holy Spirit, who leads into all truth. The world cannot receive him, because it isn’t looking for him and doesn’t recognize him. But you know him, because he lives with you now and later will be in you.
NLT.18: No, I will not abandon you as orphans — I will come to you.
NLT.19: Soon the world will no longer see me, but you will see me. Since I live, you also will live.
NLT.20: When I am raised to life again, you will know that I am in my Father, and you are in me, and I am in you.
NLT.21: Those who accept my commandments and obey them are the ones who love me. And because they love me, my Father will love them. And I will love them and reveal myself to each of them.”
NLT.22: Judas (not Judas Iscariot, but the other disciple with that name) said to him, “Lord, why are you going to reveal yourself only to us and not to the world at large?”
NLT.23: Jesus replied, “All who love me will do what I say. My Father will love them, and we will come and make our home with each of them.
NLT.24: Anyone who doesn’t love me will not obey me. And remember, my words are not my own. What I am telling you is from the Father who sent me.
NLT.25: I am telling you these things now while I am still with you.
NLT.26: But when the Father sends the Advocate as my representative — that is, the Holy Spirit — he will teach you everything and will remind you of everything I have told you.
NLT.27: “I am leaving you with a gift — peace of mind and heart. And the peace I give is a gift the world cannot give. So don’t be troubled or afraid.
NLT.28: Remember what I told you: I am going away, but I will come back to you again. If you really loved me, you would be happy that I am going to the Father, who is greater than I am.
NLT.29: I have told you these things before they happen so that when they do happen, you will believe.
NLT.30: “I don’t have much more time to talk to you, because the ruler of this world approaches. He has no power over me,
NLT.31: but I will do what the Father requires of me, so that the world will know that I love the Father. Come, let’s be going.

WEB.1: “Don’t let your heart be troubled. Believe in God. Believe also in me.
WEB.2: In my Father’s house are many homes. If it weren’t so, I would have told you. I am going to prepare a place for you.
WEB.3: If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and will receive you to myself; that where I am, you may be there also.
WEB.4: Where I go, you know, and you know the way.”
WEB.5: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we don’t know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
WEB.6: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me.
WEB.7: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also. From now on, you know him, and have seen him.”
WEB.8: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.”
WEB.9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you such a long time, and do you not know me, Philip? He who has seen me has seen the Father. How do you say, ‘Show us the Father?’
WEB.10: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father, and the Father in me? The words that I tell you, I speak not from myself; but the Father who lives in me does his works.
WEB.11: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father in me; or else believe me for the very works’ sake.
WEB.12: Most certainly I tell you, he who believes in me, the works that I do, he will do also; and he will do greater works than these, because I am going to my Father.
WEB.13: Whatever you will ask in my name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
WEB.14: If you will ask anything in my name, I will do it.
WEB.15: If you love me, keep my commandments.
WEB.16: I will pray to the Father, and he will give you another Counselor,   that he may be with you forever, —
WEB.17: the Spirit of truth, whom the world can’t receive; for it doesn’t see him, neither knows him. You know him, for he lives with you, and will be in you.
WEB.18: I will not leave you orphans. I will come to you.
WEB.19: Yet a little while, and the world will see me no more; but you will see me. Because I live, you will live also.
WEB.20: In that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
WEB.21: One who has my commandments, and keeps them, that person is one who loves me. One who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him, and will reveal myself to him.”
WEB.22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, what has happened that you are about to reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?”
WEB.23: Jesus answered him, “If a man loves me, he will keep my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him, and make our home with him.
WEB.24: He who doesn’t love me doesn’t keep my words. The word which you hear isn’t mine, but the Father’s who sent me.
WEB.25: I have said these things to you, while still living with you.
WEB.26: But the Counselor, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, he will teach you all things, and will remind you of all that I said to you.
WEB.27: Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you; not as the world gives, give I to you. Don’t let your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful.
WEB.28: You heard how I told you, ‘I go away, and I come to you.’ If you loved me, you would have rejoiced, because I said ‘I am going to my Father;’ for the Father is greater than I.
WEB.29: Now I have told you before it happens so that, when it happens, you may believe.
WEB.30: I will no more speak much with you, for the prince of the world comes, and he has nothing in me.
WEB.31: But that the world may know that I love the Father, and as the Father commanded me, even so I do. Arise, let us go from here.

CSB17.1: “Don’t let your heart be troubled. <sup>ⓘ</sup> Believe <sup>[1]</sup> in God;  <sup>ⓙ</sup> believe also in me.
CSB17.2: In my Father’s house are many rooms; if not, I would have told you. I am going away <sup>ⓚ</sup>to prepare a place for you.
CSB17.3: If I go away and prepare a place for you, I will come again <sup>ⓛ</sup>and take you to myself, so that where I am you may be also. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.4: You know the way to where I am going.” <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.5: “Lord,” Thomas <sup>ⓐ</sup> said, “we don’t know where you’re going. How can we know the way?”
CSB17.6: Jesus told him, “I am <sup>ⓑ</sup>the way, <sup>ⓒ</sup>the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
CSB17.7: If you know me, you will also know <sup>[3]</sup> my Father. <sup>ⓓ</sup>From now on you do know him <sup>ⓔ</sup> and have seen him.”
CSB17.8: “Lord,” said Philip, <sup>ⓕ</sup> “show us the Father, and that’s enough for us.”
CSB17.9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been among you all this time and you do not know me, Philip? The one who has seen me has seen the Father. <sup>ⓖ</sup>How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
CSB17.10: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? <sup>ⓗ</sup>The words I speak to you I do not speak on my own. <sup>ⓘ</sup>The Father who lives in me does his works. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.11: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me. Otherwise, believe <sup>[4]</sup>because of the works themselves. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.12: “Truly I tell you, the one who believes in me <sup>ⓛ</sup> will also do the works that I do. And he will do even greater works than these, <sup>ⓜ</sup> because I am going to the Father. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.13: Whatever you ask in my name, I will do it so that the Father may be glorified <sup>ⓐ</sup>in the Son. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.14: If you ask me <sup>[5]</sup>anything in my name, <sup>ⓒ</sup>I will do it. <sup>[6]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.15: “If you love me, you will keep <sup>[7]</sup> my commands. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Counselor <sup>[8]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup> to be with you forever.
CSB17.17: He is the Spirit <sup>ⓖ</sup> of truth. <sup>ⓗ</sup> The world is unable to receive him because it doesn’t see him or know him. But you do know him, because he remains with you and will be <sup>[9]</sup>in you. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.18: “I will not leave you as orphans; I am coming to you.
CSB17.19: In a little while the world will no longer see me, <sup>ⓙ</sup> but you will see me. <sup>ⓚ</sup>Because I live, you will live <sup>ⓛ</sup> too.
CSB17.20: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, <sup>ⓜ</sup>you are in me, and I am in you.
CSB17.21: The one who has my commands and keeps them is the one who loves me. <sup>ⓝ</sup>And the one who loves me will be loved by my Father. <sup>ⓐ</sup> I also will love him and will reveal myself to him.”
CSB17.22: Judas <sup>ⓑ</sup> (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, how is it you’re going to reveal yourself to us and not to the world?” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.23: Jesus answered, “If anyone loves me, he will keep my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.24: The one who doesn’t love me will not keep my words. The word that you hear is not mine but is from the Father who sent me. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.25: “I have spoken these things to you while I remain with you.
CSB17.26: But the Counselor, the Holy Spirit, <sup>ⓕ</sup>whom the Father will send <sup>ⓖ</sup>in my name, will teach you all things <sup>ⓗ</sup>and remind you of everything I have told you. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.27: “Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Don’t let your heart be troubled or fearful. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.28: You have heard me tell you, ‘I am going away <sup>ⓚ</sup>and I am coming to you.’ If you loved me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father, <sup>ⓛ</sup> because the Father is greater than I. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.29: I have told you now before it happens <sup>ⓝ</sup>so that when it does happen you may believe.
CSB17.30: I will not talk with you much longer, because the ruler of the world <sup>ⓐ</sup>is coming. He has no power over me. <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.31: On the contrary, so that the world may know that I love the Father, I do as the Father commanded me.  “Get up; let’s leave this place.

AMP.1: "Do not let your heart be troubled (afraid, cowardly). Believe [confidently] in God and trust in Him, [have faith, hold on to it, rely on it, keep going and] believe also in Me.
AMP.2: In My Father's house are many dwelling places. If it were not so, I would have told you, because I am going there to prepare a place for you.
AMP.3: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back again and I will take you to Myself, so that where I am you may be also.
AMP.4: And [to the place] where I am going, you know the way."
AMP.5: Thomas said to Him, "Lord, we do not know where You are going; so how can we know the way?"
AMP.6: Jesus said to him, " I am the [only] Way [to God] and the [real] Truth and the [real] Life; no one comes to the Father but through Me.
AMP.7: If you had [really] known Me, you would also have known My Father. From now on you know Him, and have seen Him."
AMP.8: Philip said to Him, "Lord, show us the Father and then we will be satisfied."
AMP.9: Jesus said to him, "Have I been with you for so long a time, and you do not know Me yet, Philip, nor recognize clearly who I am? Anyone who has seen Me has seen the Father. How can you say, 'Show us the Father?'
AMP.10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father, and the Father is in Me? The words I say to you I do not say on My own initiative or authority, but the Father, abiding continually in Me, does His works [His attesting miracles and acts of power].
AMP.11: Believe Me that I am in the Father and the Father is in Me; otherwise believe [Me] because of the [very] works themselves [which you have witnessed].
AMP.12: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, anyone who believes in Me [as Savior] will also do the things that I do; and he will do even greater things than these [in extent and outreach], because I am going to the Father.
AMP.13: And I will do whatever you ask in My name [ as My representative], this I will do, so that the Father may be glorified and celebrated in the Son.
AMP.14: If you ask Me anything in My name [as My representative], I will do it.
AMP.15: "If you [really] love Me, you will keep and obey My commandments.
AMP.16: And I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Helper (Comforter, Advocate, Intercessor—Counselor, Strengthener, Standby), to be with you forever—
AMP.17: the Spirit of Truth, whom the world cannot receive [and take to its heart] because it does not see Him or know Him, but you know Him because He (the Holy Spirit) remains with you continually and will be in you.
AMP.18: "I will not leave you as orphans [comfortless, bereaved, and helpless]; I will come [back] to you.
AMP.19: After a little while the world will no longer see Me, but you will see Me; because I live, you will live also.
AMP.20: On that day [when that time comes] you will know for yourselves that I am in My Father, and you are in Me, and I am in you.
AMP.21: The person who has My commandments and keeps them is the one who [really] loves Me; and whoever [really] loves Me will be loved by My Father, and I will love him and reveal Myself to him [I will make Myself real to him]."
AMP.22: Judas (not Iscariot) asked Him, "Lord, what has happened that You are going to reveal Yourself to us and not to the world?"
AMP.23: Jesus answered, "If anyone [really] loves Me, he will keep My word (teaching); and My Father will love him, and We will come to him and make Our dwelling place with him.
AMP.24: One who does not [really] love Me does not keep My words. And the word (teaching) which you hear is not Mine, but is the Father's who sent Me.
AMP.25: "I have told you these things while I am still with you.
AMP.26: But the Helper (Comforter, Advocate, Intercessor—Counselor, Strengthener, Standby), the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in My name [in My place, to represent Me and act on My behalf], He will teach you all things. And He will help you remember everything that I have told you.
AMP.27: Peace I leave with you; My [perfect] peace I give to you; not as the world gives do I give to you. Do not let your heart be troubled, nor let it be afraid. [Let My perfect peace calm you in every circumstance and give you courage and strength for every challenge.]
AMP.28: You heard Me tell you, 'I am going away, and I am coming back to you.' If you [really] loved Me, you would have rejoiced, because I am going [back] to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
AMP.29: I have told you now before it happens, so that when it does take place, you may believe and have faith [in Me].
AMP.30: I will not speak with you much longer, for the ruler of the world (Satan) is coming. And he has no claim on Me [no power over Me nor anything that he can use against Me];
AMP.31: but so that the world may know [without any doubt] that I love the Father, I do exactly as the Father has commanded Me [and act in full agreement with Him]. Get up, let us go from here.

MSG.1: "Don't let this throw you. You trust God, don't you? Trust me.
MSG.2: There is plenty of room for you in my Father's home. If that weren't so, would I have told you that I'm on my way to get a room ready for you?
MSG.3: And if I'm on my way to get your room ready, I'll come back and get you so you can live where I live.
MSG.4: And you already know the road I'm taking."
MSG.5: Thomas said, "Master, we have no idea where you're going. How do you expect us to know the road?"
MSG.6: Jesus said, "I am the Road, also the Truth, also the Life. No one gets to the Father apart from me.
MSG.7: If you really knew me, you would know my Father as well. From now on, you do know him. You've even seen him!"
MSG.8: Philip said, "Master, show us the Father; then we'll be content."
MSG.9: "You've been with me all this time, Philip, and you still don't understand? To see me is to see the Father. So how can you ask, 'Where is the Father?'
MSG.10: Don't you believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I speak to you aren't mere words. I don't just make them up on my own. The Father who resides in me crafts each word into a divine act.
MSG.11: "Believe me: I am in my Father and my Father is in me. If you can't believe that, believe what you see—these works.
MSG.12: The person who trusts me will not only do what I'm doing but even greater things, because I, on my way to the Father, am giving you the same work to do that I've been doing. You can count on it.
MSG.13: From now on, whatever you request along the lines of who I am and what I am doing, I'll do it. That's how the Father will be seen for who he is in the Son. I mean it.
MSG.14: Whatever you request in this way, I'll do.
MSG.15: "If you love me, show it by doing what I've told you.
MSG.16: I will talk to the Father, and he'll provide you another Friend so that you will always have someone with you.
MSG.17: This Friend is the Spirit of Truth. The godless world can't take him in because it doesn't have eyes to see him, doesn't know what to look for. But you know him already because he has been staying with you, and will even be in you!
MSG.18: "I will not leave you orphaned. I'm coming back.
MSG.19: In just a little while the world will no longer see me, but you're going to see me because I am alive and you're about to come alive.
MSG.20: At that moment you will know absolutely that I'm in my Father, and you're in me, and I'm in you.
MSG.21: "The person who knows my commandments and keeps them, that's who loves me. And the person who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and make myself plain to him."
MSG.22: Judas (not Iscariot) said, "Master, why is it that you are about to make yourself plain to us but not to the world?"
MSG.23: "Because a loveless world," said Jesus, "is a sightless world. If anyone loves me, he will carefully keep my word and my Father will love him—we'll move right into the neighborhood!
MSG.24: Not loving me means not keeping my words. The message you are hearing isn't mine. It's the message of the Father who sent me.
MSG.25: "I'm telling you these things while I'm still living with you.
MSG.26: The Friend, the Holy Spirit whom the Father will send at my request, will make everything plain to you. He will remind you of all the things I have told you.
MSG.27: I'm leaving you well and whole. That's my parting gift to you. Peace. I don't leave you the way you're used to being left—feeling abandoned, bereft. So don't be upset. Don't be distraught.
MSG.28: "You've heard me tell you, 'I'm going away, and I'm coming back.' If you loved me, you would be glad that I'm on my way to the Father because the Father is the goal and purpose of my life.
MSG.29: "I've told you this ahead of time, before it happens, so that when it does happen, the confirmation will deepen your belief in me.
MSG.30: I'll not be talking with you much more like this because the chief of this godless world is about to attack. But don't worry—he has nothing on me, no claim on me.
MSG.31: But so the world might know how thoroughly I love the Father, I am carrying out my Father's instructions right down to the last detail. "Get up. Let's go. It's time to leave here.

NIV.1:
Jesus Comforts His Disciples
“Do not let your hearts be troubled. Trust in God; trust also in me.
NIV.2: In my Father’s house are many rooms; if it were not so, I would have told you. I am going there to prepare a place for you.
NIV.3: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come back and take you to be with me that you also may be where I am.
NIV.4: You know the way to the place where I am going.”
NIV.5:
Jesus the Way to the Father
Thomas said to him, “Lord, we don’t know where you are going, so how can we know the way?”
NIV.6: Jesus answered, “I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
NIV.7: If you really knew me, you would know my Father as well. From now on, you do know him and have seen him.”
NIV.8: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father and that will be enough for us.”
NIV.9: Jesus answered: “Don’t you know me, Philip, even after I have been among you such a long time? Anyone who has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
NIV.10: Don’t you believe that I am in the Father, and that the Father is in me? The words I say to you are not just my own. Rather, it is the Father, living in me, who is doing his work.
NIV.11: Believe me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me; or at least believe on the evidence of the miracles themselves.
NIV.12: I tell you the truth, anyone who has faith in me will do what I have been doing. He will do even greater things than these, because I am going to the Father.
NIV.13: And I will do whatever you ask in my name, so that the Son may bring glory to the Father.
NIV.14: You may ask me for anything in my name, and I will do it.
NIV.15:
Jesus Promises the Holy Spirit
“If you love me, you will obey what I command.
NIV.16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Counselor to be with you forever—
NIV.17: the Spirit of truth. The world cannot accept him, because it neither sees him nor knows him. But you know him, for he lives with you and will be in you.
NIV.18: I will not leave you as orphans; I will come to you.
NIV.19: Before long, the world will not see me anymore, but you will see me. Because I live, you also will live.
NIV.20: On that day you will realize that I am in my Father, and you are in me, and I am in you.
NIV.21: Whoever has my commands and obeys them, he is the one who loves me. He who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I too will love him and show myself to him.”
NIV.22: Then Judas (not Judas Iscariot) said, “But, Lord, why do you intend to show yourself to us and not to the world?”
NIV.23: Jesus replied, “If anyone loves me, he will obey my teaching. My Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him.
NIV.24: He who does not love me will not obey my teaching. These words you hear are not my own; they belong to the Father who sent me.
NIV.25: “All this I have spoken while still with you.
NIV.26: But the Counselor, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have said to you.
NIV.27: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.
NIV.28: “You heard me say, ‘I am going away and I am coming back to you.’ If you loved me, you would be glad that I am going to the Father, for the Father is greater than I.
NIV.29: I have told you now before it happens, so that when it does happen you will believe.
NIV.30: I will not speak with you much longer, for the prince of this world is coming. He has no hold on me,
NIV.31:
but the world must learn that I love the Father and that I do exactly what my Father has commanded me.
“Come now; let us leave.

NRSVCE.1: “Do not let your hearts be troubled. Believe in God, believe also in me.
NRSVCE.2: In my Father’s house there are many dwelling places. If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?
NRSVCE.3: And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and will take you to myself, so that where I am, there you may be also.
NRSVCE.4: And you know the way to the place where I am going.”
NRSVCE.5: Thomas said to him, “Lord, we do not know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
NRSVCE.6: Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
NRSVCE.7: If you know me, you will know my Father also. From now on you do know him and have seen him.”
NRSVCE.8: Philip said to him, “Lord, show us the Father, and we will be satisfied.”
NRSVCE.9: Jesus said to him, “Have I been with you all this time, Philip, and you still do not know me? Whoever has seen me has seen the Father. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
NRSVCE.10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on my own; but the Father who dwells in me does his works.
NRSVCE.11: Believe me that I am in the Father and the Father is in me; but if you do not, then believe me because of the works themselves.
NRSVCE.12: Very truly, I tell you, the one who believes in me will also do the works that I do and, in fact, will do greater works than these, because I am going to the Father.
NRSVCE.13: I will do whatever you ask in my name, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
NRSVCE.14: If in my name you ask me for anything, I will do it.
NRSVCE.15: “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
NRSVCE.16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate, to be with you forever.
NRSVCE.17: This is the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him. You know him, because he abides with you, and he will be in you.
NRSVCE.18: “I will not leave you orphaned; I am coming to you.
NRSVCE.19: In a little while the world will no longer see me, but you will see me; because I live, you also will live.
NRSVCE.20: On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you in me, and I in you.
NRSVCE.21: They who have my commandments and keep them are those who love me; and those who love me will be loved by my Father, and I will love them and reveal myself to them.”
NRSVCE.22: Judas (not Iscariot) said to him, “Lord, how is it that you will reveal yourself to us, and not to the world?”
NRSVCE.23: Jesus answered him, “Those who love me will keep my word, and my Father will love them, and we will come to them and make our home with them.
NRSVCE.24: Whoever does not love me does not keep my words; and the word that you hear is not mine, but is from the Father who sent me.
NRSVCE.25: “I have said these things to you while I am still with you.
NRSVCE.26: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything, and remind you of all that I have said to you.
NRSVCE.27: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled, and do not let them be afraid.
NRSVCE.28: You heard me say to you, ‘I am going away, and I am coming to you.’ If you loved me, you would rejoice that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I.
NRSVCE.29: And now I have told you this before it occurs, so that when it does occur, you may believe.
NRSVCE.30: I will no longer talk much with you, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me;
NRSVCE.31: but I do as the Father has commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Rise, let us be on our way.

NET.1: “Do not let your hearts be distressed. You believe in God; believe also in me.
NET.2: There are many dwelling places in my Father’s house. Otherwise, I would have told you, because I am going away to make ready a place for you.
NET.3: And if I go and make ready a place for you, I will come again and take you to be with me, so that where I am you may be too.
NET.4: And you know the way where I am going.”
NET.5: Thomas said, “Lord, we don’t know where you are going. How can we know the way?”
NET.6: Jesus replied, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
NET.7: If you have known me, you will know my Father too. And from now on you do know him and have seen him.”
NET.8: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father, and we will be content.”
NET.9: Jesus replied, “Have I been with you for so long, and you have not known me, Philip? The person who has seen me has seen the Father! How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?
NET.10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father, and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you, I do not speak on my own initiative, but the Father residing in me performs his miraculous deeds.
NET.11: Believe me that I am in the Father, and the Father is in me, but if you do not believe me, believe because of the miraculous deeds themselves.
NET.12: I tell you the solemn truth, the person who believes in me will perform the miraculous deeds that I am doing, and will perform greater deeds than these, because I am going to the Father.
NET.13: And I will do whatever you ask in my name, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
NET.14: If you ask me anything in my name, I will do it.
NET.15: “If you love me, you will obey my commandments.
NET.16: Then I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate to be with you forever —
NET.17: the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot accept, because it does not see him or know him. But you know him, because he resides with you and will be in you.
NET.18: “I will not abandon you as orphans, I will come to you.
NET.19: In a little while the world will not see me any longer, but you will see me; because I live, you will live too.
NET.20: You will know at that time that I am in my Father and you are in me and I am in you.
NET.21: The person who has my commandments and obeys them is the one who loves me. The one who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I will love him and will reveal myself to him.”
NET.22: “Lord,” Judas (not Judas Iscariot) said, “what has happened that you are going to reveal yourself to us and not to the world?”
NET.23: Jesus replied, “If anyone loves me, he will obey my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and take up residence with him.
NET.24: The person who does not love me does not obey my words. And the word you hear is not mine, but the Father’s who sent me.
NET.25: “I have spoken these things while staying with you.
NET.26: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you everything, and will cause you to remember everything I said to you.
NET.27: “Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you; I do not give it to you as the world does. Do not let your hearts be distressed or lacking in courage.
NET.28: You heard me say to you, ‘I am going away and I am coming back to you.’ If you loved me, you would be glad that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I am.
NET.29: I have told you now before it happens, so that when it happens you may believe.
NET.30: I will not speak with you much longer, for the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me,
NET.31: but I am doing just what the Father commanded me, so that the world may know that I love the Father. Get up, let us go from here.”
